Is an enormous sink, once the bottom
Of a river, upon which the present
roadbed is now built.


Two hundred loaded cars of freight
are now tied up in the yards at Troy,
and all sidetracks between Troy and
Kalispell are filled up. Yardmen state
that all eastbound freight, excepting
that which is perishable, has been
set out west Of Spokane. At Hill
yard the yards are already full. Sew
eral cars Of stock got caught in the
trouble and unloaded at Bonners
Ferry. Considerable perishable
freight was also caught and ice is
being hauled to the scene


Assistant General Superintendent
Kennedy today gave GU the informa-
ton that the line would be tied up
at this section for at least four days
more.


The Great Northern Is only run
train now daily between st. Paul and
this city, instead Of two each way,
on account Of the food. From Spo-
kane to the coast the usual two
| trains are running.


Engine TIT. which turned over at
Leonia four days ago, is still on its
side and covered with water A steam
derrick and wrecking outfit made one


Intellectual attempt to lift 1t. but
owing to the depth of the water, no
one could go under and attach the
! tackle. The attempt was given up.
